entry.title: SAP Document Reporting Compliance Nigeria
entry.content:
Nigeria’s Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) has launched its Merchant Buyer Solution (MBS)—a PEPPOL-aligned electronic invoicing platform set to revolutionize tax compliance. Rollout begins with large taxpayers in July 2025, extending to medium and small businesses soon after.

We are working directly with the FIRS team on our SAP Document Reporting Compliance Nigeria Solution which is discussed below:

How It Works

  • ERP or API Integration: Businesses can integrate their existing Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems with the MBS via API connectors or middleware plugins. This integration ensures seamless transmission of invoices from back-end systems directly to the FIRS validation hub.
  • Invoice Generation & Validation:
    • For B2B/B2G transactions, invoices are submitted to FIRS before being shared with the buyer. The invoice undergoes validation and receives a unique IRN, CSID, and a QR codethat ensures authenticity and traceability.
    • For B2C invoices, the supplier issues the invoice immediately to the customer and must upload it to the MBS within 24 hours for reporting compliance.
    • Storage & Auditability: All validated invoices are stored in the MBS repository, where both suppliers and buyers, as well as the FIRS, can retrieve them for audit, reconciliation, or VAT verification. 

FIRS has given a number of use cases which leads to solution options for clients :

Core Use Cases & Capabilities

  • B2B / B2G Invoicing (Continuous Transaction Controls): This use case supports digital exchange of invoices between businesses or government agencies. Each invoice must be validated in real-time by the FIRS Merchant Buyer Solution (MBS) system, receiving a unique Invoice Reference Number (IRN), a Cryptographic Stamp (CSID), and a QR code for authentication.

In the above scenario's a business can choose the following:

1) Register their ERP as an access point and connect direct to the FIRS system; while this is possible during our discussions with FIRS this is not the prefered solution

2) Use a certified access point and connect your ERP to this as with other PEPPOL solution, one of FIRS goals is also to understand cross border invoice flows and by connecting to the PEPPOL network FIRS will then be reported on overseas customer invoices and overseas vendor invoices recieved via the access point.

  • Crimson-Partners Access Point is already registered in Nigeria as per our other solutions and can provide this for you
  • A company can choose their own access point and our solution will connect to this

B2C Invoicing (Near‑real‑time Reporting): Retail transactions involving consumers must be reported within 24 hours. These invoices are issued directly to customers and subsequently uploaded to the MBS platform for compliance.

  • Working with FIRS we have discussed using a consolidated invoice process daily to achieve this reporting for those with B2C sales

  • Standardized Formats & Access Points: Nigeria has adopted the UBL (Universal Business Language) XML standard to ensure interoperability. Invoices must be transmitted through licensed Access Point Providers (APPs) certified by NITDA. These APPs provide secure and traceable communication channels between senders and receivers via the PEPPOL 4-corner model. 

FIRS also provides an API to check the TIN validity of your customer before submitting invoicing accurately

✅Benefits for Nigerian Organizations

  • Improved Compliance: Real-time invoice validation eliminates the risks of underreporting or delayed VAT submissions.
  • Enhanced Transparency: The use of QR codes and digital cryptographic stamps ensures each invoice is traceable, tamper-proof, and verifiable by both buyer and tax authority.
  • Operational Efficiency: Automation reduces manual processing, lowers administrative overhead, and facilitates faster transaction processing.
  • Accurate VAT Reporting: Invoice data is automatically captured and structured for VAT compliance, reducing the need for manual declarations.

⚖ Legal Mandate & Penalties
The implementation of electronic invoicing is legally mandated by the FIRS under the Finance Act, supported by regulatory frameworks from NITDA and NCC. Non-compliance can lead to penalties including monetary fines, interest on underreported taxes, and restrictions on eligibility for input VAT claims.

� Rollout Strategy & Timeline

  • July 2025: Mandatory for large taxpayers as classified by FIRS.
  • Q4 2025: Gradual onboarding of medium-sized businesses.
  • 2026: Full rollout to small businesses and B2C operations.

Crimson provide a standard SAP Document Reporting Compliance Solution, a full staging area for testing, training and handbooks for your organisation

Benefits :

  • Single Global Process using Document Reporting Compliance
  • E2E compliant solution built with the FIRS team
  • Generation of electronic invoices in XML format (FI, SD) for B2B and B2G
  • Self-billing scenario's
  • FICA and RE-FX billing
  • Manual or automated invoice submission
  • Receiving of e-invoices in XML format and Integration to VIM Software
  • Human Readable PDF in Cockpit for incoming invoices

� Next Steps

  • Confirm your registration with FIRS and verify your Tax Identification Number (TIN).
  • Engage with a licensed Access Point Provider (APP) or Software Integrator (SI) to assist with system integration such as Crimson who can meet both these
  • Prepare your ERP systems or point-of-sale platforms for API connectivity and XML invoice generation.
  • Conduct end-to-end testing with FIRS and ensure your teams are trained in the e-invoicing process and legal requirements. 

� Bottom Line
The FIRSMBS e-invoicing system marks a major milestone in Nigeria’s digital tax transformation. It also marks a shift for African countries with other African nations choosing highly complex solutions, Nigeria has chosen a global standard and is following in similar fashion to Malaysia, UAE and other upcoming mandates.

It ensures:

  • Real-time transparency
  • Reduced invoicing errors
  • Automated VAT reporting
  • Lower compliance costs

Compatible with ECC, S/4HANA & S/4 Cloud, this is another step on the journey of global electronic invoicing across borders.

Look out next for our Kenya, Zambia, Ghana solution and BTP Application!

Crimson Tax Tech | FIRS e-Invoicing Advisory

entry.title: Re: Issue in developing GSTR2 report
entry.content:
Hello @sapcomyogik 

This is a common issue when developing tax reports in S/4HANA Public Cloud, especially for GSTR2, due to the different data models used in

  • I_JournalEntryItem contains general ledger items, including tax lines, but tax amounts can be merged or distributed based on document structure.
  • I_OperationalAcctDocTaxItem is designed to extract tax-specific information (e.g., Tax Codes, Tax Base, Tax Amount) in alignment with transactional tax reporting requirements like GSTR2.

Recommendation

Use the Journal Entry Header (I_JournalEntry) as your primary link between both CDS Views via the JournalEntry and CompanyCode fields.

  • Ensure you join I_OperationalAcctDocTaxItem based on JournalEntry and CompanyCode, then map tax amounts by TaxCode, TaxBaseAmount, or TaxAmount.
  • Keep in mind that not all tax amounts shown in accounting lines (I_JournalEntryItem) will directly match tax amounts in the tax-specific CDS unless aligned through correct joins and aggregation logic.

SAP Help references-Tax Reporting and CDS View Documentation

For accurate GSTR2 reporting,SAP recommended to build your logic primarily from I_OperationalAcctDocTaxItem, as this view reflects tax amounts from the operational perspective, ensuring better alignment with statutory reports.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Cumulative/YTD load for FI to Group Reporting Integra...
entry.content:
@Eyal_Feiler  I agree with your observation—while ECCS offers various options, it's important to note that SAP Group Reporting (GR) is a periodic system where data is stored in periodic mode.

As you rightly pointed out, GRDC is currently out of scope and not preferred. However, we would still recommend utilizing GRDC as an interim solution until the 2025 upgrade.

Post-upgrade, you will have access to the Release Universal Journal – Support for Prior Periods functionality, which enables both full and delta mode processing for earlier periods.

Alternatively, you may also consider leveraging the standard API or the flexible upload functionality, based on your system landscape and data integration needs.

Otheroption : release the data every period and run the consolidation scenarios once in a year P12/P16.   I don't recommend the GRPL- YTD realignment. this will blast the volumes in the Universal Journal.

entry.title: Integration with Third Party PLM Systems
entry.content:
In our first blog, Unify, Innovate, Excel, we introduced SAP’s strategic vision for Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) — a unified, integrated approach to manage product data, processes, and collaboration across the enterprise. In our second blog, we explored how this strategy plays out in discrete industries, focusing on the tight integration between engineering tools, BOM transitions, and cross-functional collaboration.  In our third blog, we shifted our focus on how our strategy for process industries addresses their unique challenges in managing recipe data, ensuring compliance, enabling collaboration, and transitioning smoothly from development to execution. We will now wrap up our discussion with the last blog in our series, turning our focus towards those customers who use external PLM solutions to manage their product development processes with the need to smoothly transition product data from development to execution to establish an integrated digital thread for the enterprise.

Understanding the PLM Ecosystem

Product Lifecycle Management is not a new concept; companies have been looking for ways to continuously streamline their design to operate processes while software providers have been delivering solutions to meet these customer needs. As a result, there are customers who have taken a decision to invest in external PLM systems like Siemens Teamcenter, Dassault Systèmes 3DEXPERIENCE, PTC Windchill or Autodesk Vault to store product design details coming from various design authoring tools. Adding to the complexity of the situation, the same customer may have more than one of these solutions in place, depending upon the given industry (aerospace and defense, for example) and a customer’s acquisition history. In the end, all these customers have the need to have their product design information seamlessly flow from these external PLM tools into the ERP environments to streamline supply chain process.

SAP is well-equipped to support customers with a reliable, standards-based integration solution that centralizes product data within the SAP Business Suite. This facilitates efficient data sharing across the value chain and enables effective bi-directional change management for organizations.

As a result, customers can preserve their existing investments in their PLM technology, reduce their total cost of ownership with out-of-the-box integration, and leverage additional SAP capabilities to accommodate hybrid scenarios like supplier collaboration and visualization, tailored to their business needs.

Let’s briefly look at a few key areas on how SAP’s solution, known as PLM system integration, enables interoperation between SAP and external PLM systems to enable efficient product data flows and better collaboration.

Efficient Pipeline for Product Data

First and foremost, it is a necessity that any successful integration provides the ability to exchange data between systems. SAP’s goal is not only to provide this but ultimately to boost efficiency and collaboration within an organization with bi-directional integrations for unified product data across the entire supply chain.

When considering data, what needs to be passed is not just limited to parts, and bills of materials, but also needs to include additional product relevant data necessary for change management, variant definitions, vendor data, production operations, inspection characteristics and others.  When the breadth of the data is expanded to include all these facets, it becomes possible to support a wide range of business process scenarios (MTS, MTO, ETO, CTO, CTO+) with the product data.

PLM system integration not only handles these data needs, but it does so by using the taxonomies used to describe product data in the different departments within a company.   For example, the solution understands that in engineering an "object" might be called a part or an item, but in SAP the manufacturing and purchasing understand that same "object" as a material master.   Having this common taxonomy framework in place ensures that enabling the integration is not delayed due to communication issues.

PLM system integration performs the data exchange between SAP and the external PLM systems, through highly performant and automatic process triggers - meaning data in passed in context and when it is required.  When data is transferred and handled in the proper sequence (for example, in SAP materials need to be created before adding to them to a bill of material), we ensure a unified and complete view of the data is available in both SAP and the external PLM system.  This improves data quality and transparency across the entire supply chain.

End to End Business Process Integration

While seamlessly transferring data is important, what drives true value for customers is the ability to integrate PLM systems such as Teamcenter, 3DEXPERIENCE, PTC Windchill, and Autodesk Vault to support end-to-end business processes.

What do we mean by end-to-end business processes? As mentioned above, customers need to support a wide range of business process scenarios (MTS, MTO, ETO, CTO, CTO+). When considering the different needs of these processes, PLM system integration supports the integration of data within the context of end to end business processes (Supported Business Processes).

To enable this, PLM system integration comes with standard content in the form of Business Configuration Sets to enable the easy integration of SAP to an external PLM system.   You can use this content for the initial configuration of your SAP system, and you can change and extend the configuration wherever necessary, to fit your needs.  

Efficient and Transparent Data Management 

We discussed this topic during our second blog, but it is worth mentioning again in the context of bringing product data into SAP via PLM system integration.  There is always the need for product design information to be made available to the supply chain for downstream processes.   For example, manufacturing needs a manufacturing bill of material (mBOM) to support production operations, but engineering only provides their view of the product in the form of an engineering bill of material (eBOM).  

Integrating external PLM systems with SAP simplifies the transition from eBOM to mBOM by seamlessly transferring engineering data from an external PLM system into SAP. SAP’s capabilities can then convert the eBOM to mBOM while keeping structures aligned and under change control. Additionally, visualization data linked to business data can be easily created, supporting downstream processes like service management, asset repair, and spare parts ordering. These are opportunities to boost production readiness, reduce rework, and improve efficiency.

By building a seamless connection between engineering and other departments—such as manufacturing, sales, sourcing, or service— we improve product quality, maintain consistency amid changes, and enable the reuse of engineering data for the enterprise. This positions us to bring new products to market more swiftly.

What does the future hold?

While the future is hard to predict, we know the one constant in the world is change.  As customer processes change and as industries continue to evolve, SAP is poised to help navigate the challenges and opportunities ahead. By continuing to optimize and enhance the capabilities provided for integration to external PLM tools, SAP addresses both current industry needs and future demands.

With the focus on seamlessly integrating product data across engineering, sourcing, manufacturing, quality, service, etc., SAP helps deliver an integrated digital thread across the organization where not only is the product data integrated, but the processes are integrated as well.   This empowers organizations to reduce errors, improve agility, and meet the growing need for sustainable, high-quality products, not only now, but in the future as well!

To learn more about our strategy, solution capabilities, and future innovations for PLM system integration, please visit the following links: 

  • Strategy blog: SAP Business Suite – The Integrated Powerhouse for Seamless Product Lifecycle Management
  • Strategy blog: Collaborative Product Development in Discrete Industries

entry.title: Modify SFTP Report
entry.content:
Good afternoon, everyone!

I have a question concerning an FTP report configuration that we have set up in our LMS. We have a User Item Status CSV report that is being exported to one of our teams. However, the report is exporting the item status for all items in our LMS. The team that receives this report is requesting that the export file only include information for specific items/courses, and that the information is sent is a specific format. Is there a way to modify the FTP configuration to only export the information for a specific set of items/courses? Or will we have to stop the FTP export of the report and manually schedule a recurring report with only the specific items/courses? I hope my question makes sense.

Below is the report system configuration that we currently have. This is what I am wanting to modify to only pull specific items. Is this possible?

# for a specific report, add
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.protocol=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.server=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.port=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.path=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.userI
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.password=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.timeout=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.enablePassiveMode=

entry.title: Custo Padrão
entry.content:
Olá, boa tarde!

Sou recém-chegado à comunidade SAP.

Fizemos recentemente a migração do SAP ECC para o SAP S/4 HANA.

Na versão ECC o custo padrão gerado mensalmente era sofria alteração, creio eu, ser em função da movimentação do material durante o período.

Já na versão S/4 Hana, o custo padrão não está mais sofrendo alteração, desde janeiro, quando foi feito a virada de sistema, o custo padrão permanece o mesmo valor.

Alguém saberia esclarecer essa questão?

Desde já, obrigado.

entry.title: Supply Chain Logistics Maturity Model: Gaining Traction & Leveraging Visibility at Level 2 (3 of 5)
entry.content:
For companies that have moved beyond purely manual processes and have implemented a basic transportation management system (TMS), the Supply Chain Logistics Maturity Model places them around Level 2, "Somewhat Digitalized". At this level, you have core capabilities like load consolidation and booking management in place, but with limited point-to-point connections with a few partners, likely via traditional EDI. Nonetheless, communication often still defaults to emails and phone calls, especially when circumstances deviate from plans and annual  contracts. Visibility into routes, rates, shipment status, and up-to-date ETAs are available, but often require logging into and navigating through multiple external portals for each carrier, separate from your core order management systems and fulfillment processes.

Companies at Level 2 have begun their digital journey, but key limitations remain:

  • TMS processes cover load consolidation, tendering, and booking with limited carrier collaboration.
  • Limited visibility into shipment status and risk derived from multiple external portals disconnected from fulfillment.
  • Some cross-functional integration and proactivity but gaps preventing process optimization and digital collaboration.

At this stage, you have some pieces of the puzzle digitalized, but they aren't fully connected, and therefore fail to provide the comprehensive, real-time insights needed for truly proactive decision-making.  For this reason, the primary focus for improvement shifts to leveraging logistics visibility and process integration to improve agility.

Level 2 companies must focus on connecting more trading partners digitally, while maintaining efficiency at scale, and integrating visibility data directly into their logistics systems as well as the order management processes of their ERP. Moving beyond limited point-to-point connections to a business network becomes essential for scalable collaboration. This enhanced connectivity enables more near real-time data exchange for processes like tendering, ordering, and tracking, allowing companies to avoid chasing email and stopping to log into external portals. By integrating visibility into internal systems, you can start identifying issues in the context of your business, and addressing them along your embedded processes before they become major disruptions, setting the stage for greater resilience.

Return for the next installment that dives into how companies at Level 3 build on this foundation to become truly connected and proactive.   (prior post in this series) 

Read more about logistics visibility:

What is logistics visibility?

Inbound Logistics magazine: 10 Tips to Effective Logistics Visibility

Why choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace over individual visibility platforms?

               Brief

               SAP Community post series

Product Tour: SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

entry.title: I always had one doubt in my mind that how people are so dramatic about marketing
entry.content:
What I love must about marketing is that I get to make people fall in love with brands.

I remember being on one of my first college classes, it was “Introduction to marketing or something like that, can’t quite remember. The point is that our teacher was just explaining what marketers job really is.

Her statement went as follows: “You know how doctors heal people, architects design buildings and houses? Well, us marketers, make people fall in love with brands”

Those words stuck in my head from that moment back in 2011. They made me love marketing from that moment on.

I get to work with amazing brands and people. I see how much effort and love people put into creating a brand, I get to help them not only get their brand (something they feel so proud of) out there but also help them grow their business. There is not a more rewarding feeling than seeing someone succeed through your help.

entry.title: Architecture Gone Wrong: Leveraging SAP Architecture Center for Better Outcomes
entry.content:
In the today growing software development, architecture plays a crucial role in determining the success and efficiency of a system. Poor architecture can lead to significant costs, performance issues, and maintenance headaches.

This blog explores the pitfalls of bad architecture and highlights the advantages of leveraging the SAP Architecture Center to ensure best practices and cost savings.

The Pitfalls of Poor Architecture

1. High Costs: Poorly designed architecture can lead to increased costs in several ways:

  • Maintenance: Systems with bad architecture require frequent fixes and updates, leading to higher maintenance costs.
  • Scalability: Inefficient architecture can struggle to scale, necessitating expensive overhauls or replacements.
  • Performance: Suboptimal architecture can result in slow performance, requiring additional resources to meet performance standards.

2. Performance Issues: Bad architecture can severely impact system performance:

  • Latency: Inefficient data flow and processing can lead to high latency, affecting user experience.
  • Reliability: Poor architecture can result in frequent downtimes and failures, reducing system reliability.
  • Resource Utilization: Inefficient use of resources can lead to higher operational costs and lower performance.

The SAP Architecture Center: A Solution

The SAP Architecture Center offers a comprehensive suite of tools, best practices, and reference architectures to help businesses design efficient and cost-effective systems

https://architecture.learning.sap.com 

Here are some key benefits:

1. Best Practices: The SAP Architecture Center provides access to industry best practices, ensuring that your architecture is designed to meet the highest standards. This includes guidelines for security, performance, and scalability.

2. Reference Architectures: Reference architectures are templates that outline the interactions between various services and components. They provide a standardized approach to designing systems, ensuring optimal configurations and seamless integration 

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchCatalog/ 

3. Community-Driven Guidance: The SAP Architecture Center fosters a community of practice where architects and developers can share insights, collaborate, and learn from each other. This collaborative environment helps in adopting proven frameworks and innovative solutions

https://architecture.learning.sap.com/community/intro 

Advantages and Benefits

1. Cost Savings: By following SAP best practices and utilizing reference architectures, businesses can significantly reduce costs:

  • Reduced Maintenance: Well-designed architecture requires less frequent fixes and updates.
  • Efficient Resource Utilization: Optimal configurations ensure efficient use of resources, reducing operational costs.
  • Scalability: Scalable architecture minimizes the need for expensive overhauls.

2. Improved Performance: Leveraging the SAP Architecture Center can lead to better system performance:

  • Lower Latency: Efficient data flow and processing reduce latency.
  • Higher Reliability: Robust architecture ensures fewer downtimes and failures.
  • Optimal Resource Utilization: Efficient use of resources leads to better performance.

FinOps for Cost Saving

Financial Operations (FinOps) is a practice that combines financial management with cloud operations to optimize costs. Integrating FinOps with SAP best practices can further enhance cost savings:

  1. Cost Monitoring: FinOps tools help in monitoring cloud spending, identifying cost drivers, and optimizing resource usage.
  2. Budgeting and Forecasting: FinOps practices enable accurate budgeting and forecasting, ensuring that financial resources are allocated efficiently.
  3. Continuous Improvement: FinOps encourages continuous improvement by regularly reviewing and optimizing cloud spending, leading to sustained cost savings.

Conclusion

Poor architecture can lead to significant costs and performance issues. However, by leveraging the SAP Architecture Center and adopting FinOps practices, businesses can design efficient, cost-effective, and high-performing systems. The SAP Architecture Center provides best practices, reference architectures, and community-driven guidance to ensure that your architecture is built to the highest standards. Embrace these tools and practices to avoid the pitfalls of bad architecture and achieve optimal results.

entry.title: Re: Custom fields not updating BAPI_PO_CHANGE extensionin
entry.content:
It looks like you are passing the values of the custom fields as offset data in the VALUEPART1 field of the EXTENSIONIN parameter.

If so, I believe you would need to copy those values from this structure into matching fields of EKPO using a badi implementation.

Take a look at the method EXTENSIONIN of the badi ME_BAPI_PO_IBS. I think you need to fill your custom fields in POITEM as the data from function module BAPI_PO_CHANGE is processed.

Did you add your custom fields to structure MEPOITEM as well as to EKPO?

entry.title: Re: Custom fields not updating BAPI_PO_CHANGE extensionin
entry.content: Method EXTENSIONIN of Badi ME_BAPI_PO_CUST might be a better choice.

entry.title: Re: S4/HANA - How to develop an SAPUI5 app calling a back...
entry.content:
By definition, a SAPUI5 app is a web app so it runs in the browser, regardless if’s consuming data from S/4HANA, ECC, CRM or any other SAP or non SAP system. 
as the app runs in user’s browser, you cannot select data like in an classic ABAP application (I guess this is what you mean by “ECC” in your question).

so, you need a REST API - regardless it’s OData or not. By if your backend is an S/4HANA system creating an API using ABAP (Using RAP for recent versions specially) is the easiest and default choice

entry.title: LMS Data Story Report
entry.content:
I am trying to create a story report based on LMS data that shows the total amount of time spent in courses by our employees. I am summing on the duration field. However, many employees have been assigned a course multiple times, despite completing it only once so the duration is being overstated when added. 

For example, I have records like:

Employee#Course NameAssignment DateCompletion DateDuration
1234Course ABCJan. 1, 2025May 31, 202510
1234 Course ABCFeb. 15, 2025May 31, 202510
1234Course ABCMar. 15, 2025May 31, 202510

In the above example, the employee completed the course once and so I want to show the duration as 10, not 30. Does anyone know of a way of accomplishing this? Is there a way to filter the query or the table on the max of completion date for the same employee id and course name?

entry.title: Suggestions to execute mass stock relocation without manually creating a WT for each item
entry.content:
We are relocating stock within the same warehouse. The relocation involves new storage types and bins but within the same warehouse number.

We want to:

Physically move all materials from old bins to new ones

Record article numbers, quantities, base unit of measure, and new vs old storage bins

Avoid creating individual warehouse tasks manually for each article

We are seeking guidance on the following:

Which SAP Fiori application should we use in S/4HANA Cloud to perform this kind of mass stock transfer?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Suggestions to execute mass stock relocation without...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In addition to the Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/mass-stock-relocation-in-warehouse-without-creating-individual-warehouse/qaq-p/14132251

I would recommend checking the ‘Transfer Stock – In-Plant’ app provides functionality for transferring stock between storage locations or bins within the same plant. It offers a guided process where the user can select materials, specify source and target storage locations, and confirm quantities to be transferred. 

Also, I would recommend checking the ‘Post Goods Movement’ app, that allows users to perform a wide range of goods movements, including goods receipts, issues, and transfer postings. It offers flexibility for different movement types and is often used in processes that involve adjustments, consumption postings, or more complex movement scenarios. 

However, neither app provides standard functionality to import an Excel file and automatically process multiple stock transfers. For bulk processing, additional development or automation would be necessary.

In addition, 

For more information:

  • Transfer Stock – In-Plant (SAP Fiori Apps Library)

  • Transfer Stock – In-Plant (SAP Help Portal)

  • Post Goods Movement (SAP Fiori Apps Library)

  • Post Goods Movement (SAP Help Portal)

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Variable pay based on different base dates in prorated template
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a requirement where in bonus period is 1/Aug/2024 – 31/July/2025. If the user doesn’t have any change in salary after 1st October, then the 1st October salary should be used for the entire year but if the user has any salary change after 1st October(offcycle increase) then the system should calculate as per the dates. Below is the sample data.

RQ2 and RQ3 will work as expected with standard proration is my understanding but to achieve RQ1 do we have any standard solution from SAP

We thought of a solution by creating a MDF object and populate all the salaries from 1st Aug till July 31st and update all the 1st Aug till 31st  Sep salary to October 1st salary value in MDF object for users who don’t have a salary change after 1st October and use this value in the VP history, Since this involves a lot a manual process, could you please let us know if we have a standard process to achieve this behavior in a EC Variable pay template.

Kindly provide details if anyone has experience implementing such a requirement

 

Req No

Business Requirement

Date

Date

Date

Date

Date

Expectation

 

 

1-Aug

1-Sep

1-Oct

1-May

31-Jul

 

RQ1

Standard

100,000 

100,000 

105,000 

105,000 

105,000

We use $105,000 for the full 365 period

RQ2

Out of Cycle 1

100,000 

100,000 

105,000

110,000

110,000

We use $100,000 for August and September, $105,000 for October to April, $110,000 for May to July assignment

RQ3

Out of Cycle 2

100,000 

110,000 

110,000 

110,000 

110,000

We use $100,000 for August, $110,000 for the second rest of year assignment

Regards,

Siva

entry.title: Re: Modify SFTP Report

I hope this makes sense.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Application Studio / Delete Workspace
entry.content:
Hi Matthias,

To delete the unused workspaces:

1. First open the BAS and click on the 3 lines symbol then File > Open Folder  

2.Give your path to projects folder. Generally for everyone the path would be the same :/home/user/projects

And open till projects path only!

3. Then Select the projects folder. 

4.Then you can find your projects & workspaces 

5.Select the workspace which you want to delete and right click on it. You can find the option "Delete Permanently"


Note : Make sure you are doing this in the explorer but not in any workspace 


I hope this solves your issue.

Thanks,

Karthik

entry.title: Re: Log of changes in Material Master EWM
entry.content:
Check transaction /SCWM/PROD_CD_READ

 

You can check customizing (activation) here in spro IMG :

Extended Warehouse Management > Master Data > Product > Activate Change Documents.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM ABAP - RF Device Cookie (or any mechanism to track individual devices)
entry.content:
how do you use RF? with its? with no GUI? 

Regardless of the technology, RF ist usually moved around and if you have portable printer it is usually on the assigned to resource. And something to be print in EWM RF you have location related things available, like activity area, or storage type or work center, or resource. so usually it is enough business objects to assign printer. Could you give more information about process and why you have printer input in rf, which you want to link to device.

entry.title: Re: Issue in PO Price Determination After OMET Configurat...
entry.content: Dear sir, I made control, so that user can not create PO without PR, but after this config in OMET Previously, when users created a PO without referencing a PR, the system would automatically fetch the last PO price during PO creation. However, after implementing the OMET control, this functionality appears to have stopped — and the price is no longer being fetched automatically, even when a valid source of historical pricing exists

entry.title: UPLOAD DATA FROM EXCEL TO ANY TABLE Z IN SAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I'm locking for FM or anyway for check validate field about DATS, TIMS, CURR, CURK,... and Value Range in internal table because I want to upload data from excel to any Z table data. So I need help to validate data before upload into internal table Z*. Here is my code correct and can upload any table Z but it doesn't validate data to internal table.

FORM UPLOAD_DATA_FROM_EXCEL.
  DATA: LT_FILE_TABLE    TYPE FILETABLE,
        LV_RC            TYPE I,
        LV_FILE_PATH     TYPE STRING,
        LV_FILE_LENGTH   TYPE I,
        LV_XSTRING       TYPE XSTRING,
        LT_RAW_DATA      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF X255,
        LT_EXCEL_DATA    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ALSMEX_TABLINE,
        LS_EXCEL_LINE    TYPE ALSMEX_TABLINE,
        LT_KEY_FIELDS    TYPE TABLE OF DD03L,
        LV_ROW           TYPE I VALUE 1,
        LV_ERROR_COUNT   TYPE I VALUE 0,
        LV_SUCCESS_COUNT TYPE I VALUE 0,
        LV_MSG           TYPE STRING.

  TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_FIELD_MAPPING,
           EXCEL_NAME TYPE STRING,
           DDIC_NAME  TYPE FIELDNAME,
           COL_POS    TYPE I, " Excel column position
           DATATYPE   TYPE DATATYPE_D, " Add datatype to mapping
         END OF TY_FIELD_MAPPING.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <LT_ALV>      TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
                 <LS_ALV>      TYPE ANY,
                 <LS_EXISTING> TYPE ANY,
                 <LV_FIELD>    TYPE ANY,
                 <LV_IS_NEW>   TYPE ANY,
                 <LT_CELLTAB>  TYPE LVC_T_STYL,
                 <LS_CELLTAB>  TYPE LVC_S_STYL,
                 <LV_LOCKED>   TYPE ANY.

  DATA: LT_FILE_CONTENT TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TEXT255,
        LV_ERROR_TEXT   TYPE STRING,
        LV_MSG_TYPE     TYPE SY-MSGTY,
        LV_MSG_ID       TYPE SY-MSGID,
        LV_MSG_NO       TYPE SY-MSGNO,
        LV_MSG_V1       TYPE SY-MSGV1.

  "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 1. Upload Excel file content
  "-----------------------------------------------------
  CALL METHOD CL_GUI_FRONTEND_SERVICES=>FILE_OPEN_DIALOG
    EXPORTING
      WINDOW_TITLE            = 'Select Excel File'
      FILE_FILTER             = 'Excel Files (*.XLSX)|*.XLSX|'
    CHANGING
      FILE_TABLE              = LT_FILE_TABLE
      RC                      = LV_RC
    EXCEPTIONS
      FILE_OPEN_DIALOG_FAILED = 1
      CNTL_ERROR              = 2
      ERROR_NO_GUI            = 3
      NOT_SUPPORTED_BY_GUI    = 4
      OTHERS                  = 5.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0 OR LV_RC = 0 OR LT_FILE_TABLE IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'File selection was cancelled' TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'W'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  READ TABLE LT_FILE_TABLE INDEX 1 INTO DATA(LS_FILE).
  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Could not read selected file information' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  LV_FILE_PATH = LS_FILE-FILENAME.

  "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 2. Process Excel content with improved header validation
  "-----------------------------------------------------
  DATA: LV_FILENAME TYPE RLGRAP-FILENAME.
  LV_FILENAME = LV_FILE_PATH.

  " First read only the header row (row 1)
  CALL FUNCTION 'ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE'
    EXPORTING
      FILENAME                = LV_FILENAME
      I_BEGIN_COL             = 1
      I_BEGIN_ROW             = 1
      I_END_COL               = 100
      I_END_ROW               = 1
    TABLES
      INTERN                  = LT_EXCEL_DATA
    EXCEPTIONS
      INCONSISTENT_PARAMETERS = 1
      UPLOAD_OLE              = 2
      OTHERS                  = 3.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0 OR LT_EXCEL_DATA IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Could not read header from Excel file' TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Get the expected fields from data dictionary
  DATA: LT_EXPECTED_FIELDS TYPE TABLE OF DFIES,
        LT_ACTUAL_HEADERS  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF STRING,
        LS_HEADER_LINE     TYPE ALSMEX_TABLINE,
        LV_EXPECTED_FIELD  TYPE DFIES-FIELDNAME,
        LV_HEADER_MISSING  TYPE ABAP_BOOL VALUE ABAP_FALSE,
        LT_FIELD_MAPPING   TYPE TABLE OF TY_FIELD_MAPPING,
        LS_FIELD_MAPPING   TYPE TY_FIELD_MAPPING,
        LT_MISSING_FIELDS  TYPE TABLE OF STRING,
        LT_EXTRA_FIELDS    TYPE TABLE OF STRING.

  CALL FUNCTION 'DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET'
    EXPORTING
      TABNAME        = P_TABLE
    TABLES
      DFIES_TAB      = LT_EXPECTED_FIELDS
    EXCEPTIONS
      NOT_FOUND      = 1
      INTERNAL_ERROR = 2
      OTHERS         = 3.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Could not retrieve expected table fields' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

    "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 2. Process Excel content with STRICT header validation
  "-----------------------------------------------------
  DATA:
    lv_error_msg       TYPE string,
    lv_header_mismatch TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_false.

  " Extract headers from Excel (row 1)
  CLEAR lt_actual_headers.
  LOOP AT lt_excel_data INTO ls_header_line WHERE row = 1.
    IF ls_header_line-value IS INITIAL.
      lv_error_msg = 'Empty header found in column ' && ls_header_line-col && '. All headers must have a value.'.
      lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.
    APPEND ls_header_line-value TO lt_actual_headers.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Check for empty headers
  IF lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
    MESSAGE lv_error_msg TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Validate number of columns
  IF lines( lt_actual_headers ) <> lines( lt_expected_fields ).
    lv_error_msg = |Number of headers ({ lines( lt_actual_headers ) }) does not match target fields ({ lines( lt_expected_fields ) })|.
    MESSAGE lv_error_msg TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " STRICT ORDER VALIDATION - Check each header name and position
  LOOP AT lt_expected_fields INTO DATA(ls_expected_field).
    DATA(lv_index) = sy-tabix.

    " Read corresponding Excel header
    READ TABLE lt_actual_headers INTO DATA(lv_header) INDEX lv_index.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      lv_error_msg = |Header at position { lv_index } is missing|.
      lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.

    " Case-insensitive comparison with exact position check
    IF to_upper( lv_header ) <> to_upper( ls_expected_field-fieldname ).
      lv_error_msg = |Header at position { lv_index } is incorrect. Found: "{ lv_header }", Expected: "{ ls_expected_field-fieldname }"|.
      lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " If any mismatch, report error and stop
  IF lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
    MESSAGE lv_error_msg TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    EXIT.
  ENDIF.

  " Build field mapping between Excel columns and DDIC fields
  CLEAR LT_EXCEL_DATA.
  " Now read the entire Excel file (starting from row 2 for data)
  CALL FUNCTION 'ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE'
    EXPORTING
      FILENAME                = LV_FILENAME
      I_BEGIN_COL             = 1
      I_BEGIN_ROW             = 2 " Skip header row
      I_END_COL               = 100
      I_END_ROW               = 9999
    TABLES
      INTERN                  = LT_EXCEL_DATA
    EXCEPTIONS
      INCONSISTENT_PARAMETERS = 1
      UPLOAD_OLE              = 2
      OTHERS                  = 3.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0 OR LT_EXCEL_DATA IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Could not read data from Excel file' TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 3. Get key fields from DD03L and also field types for date/time conversion
  SELECT * FROM DD03L INTO TABLE LT_KEY_FIELDS
    WHERE TABNAME = P_TABLE
      AND KEYFLAG = 'X'
      AND AS4LOCAL = 'A'
      AND AS4VERS = '0000'
    ORDER BY POSITION.

  " Get all fields with their data types for date/time conversion
  DATA: LT_FIELD_TYPES TYPE TABLE OF DFIES.
  CALL FUNCTION 'DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET'
    EXPORTING
      TABNAME        = P_TABLE
    TABLES
      DFIES_TAB      = LT_FIELD_TYPES
    EXCEPTIONS
      NOT_FOUND      = 1
      INTERNAL_ERROR = 2
      OTHERS         = 3.

  " 4. Assign ALV data to field symbol
  ASSIGN GT_ALV_DATA->* TO <LT_ALV>.
  IF <LT_ALV> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
    MESSAGE 'Cannot access ALV data' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " 5. Create structure for key comparison
  TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE,
           KEY_STRING TYPE STRING,
           ROW_INDEX  TYPE I,
         END OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE.

  DATA: LT_EXISTING_KEYS TYPE HASHED TABLE OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE
                         WITH UNIQUE KEY KEY_STRING,
        LT_NEW_KEYS      TYPE HASHED TABLE OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE
                         WITH UNIQUE KEY KEY_STRING,
        LS_KEY           TYPE TY_KEY_STRUCTURE,
        LT_DUPLICATES    TYPE TABLE OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE.
  " 6. Process Excel data with date/time conversion
  DATA: LV_CURRENT_ROW TYPE I VALUE 0.
  DATA: LV_DATE_STR       TYPE STRING,
        LV_TIME_STR       TYPE STRING,
        LV_CONVERTED_DATE TYPE STRING,
        LV_CONVERTED_TIME TYPE STRING.

  LOOP AT LT_EXCEL_DATA INTO LS_EXCEL_LINE.
    " New row handling
    IF LS_EXCEL_LINE-ROW <> LV_CURRENT_ROW.
      LV_CURRENT_ROW = LS_EXCEL_LINE-ROW.
      LV_ROW = LV_ROW + 1.

      " Add new line to ALV
      APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_ALV>.

      " Mark as new record
      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_NEW' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_IS_NEW>.
      IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
        <LV_IS_NEW> = ABAP_TRUE.
      ENDIF.

      " Initialize celltab for styling
      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CELLTAB' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LT_CELLTAB>.
      IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
        CLEAR <LT_CELLTAB>.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_EXCEL_LINE-COL OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
      <LV_FIELD> = LS_EXCEL_LINE-VALUE.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " 7. Validate keys for new records and handle duplicates by updating existing records
  DATA: LV_START_INDEX     TYPE I,
        LV_END_INDEX       TYPE I,
        LV_DUPLICATE_FOUND TYPE ABAP_BOOL VALUE ABAP_FALSE,
        LV_KEY_MATCH       TYPE ABAP_BOOL,
        LV_UPDATE_COUNT    TYPE I VALUE 0,
        LV_INSERT_COUNT    TYPE I VALUE 0.

  LV_START_INDEX = LINES( <LT_ALV> ) - LV_ROW + 2.
  LV_END_INDEX = LINES( <LT_ALV> ).

  " First pass: check for duplicate primary keys
  LOOP AT <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_ALV> FROM LV_START_INDEX TO LV_END_INDEX.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_NEW' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_IS_NEW>.
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_IS_NEW> = ABAP_TRUE.
      " Check for duplicate keys in existing data
      LOOP AT <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_EXISTING>.
        " Skip new records being added
        IF SY-TABIX BETWEEN LV_START_INDEX AND LV_END_INDEX.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_DELETED' OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_DELETED>).
        IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_EXISTING_DELETED> <> 'X'.
          LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_TRUE.

          " Check each key field
          LOOP AT LT_KEY_FIELDS INTO DATA(LS_KEY_FIELD_NEW).
            ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_KEY_FIELD_NEW-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_KEY_FIELD_NEW-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_FIELD>).
            IF <LV_FIELD> <> <LV_EXISTING_FIELD>.
              LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_FALSE.
              EXIT.
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.

          " If keys match, update the existing record with new data
          IF LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_TRUE.
            " Get all non-key fields from the new record
*            LOOP AT LT_FIELD_TYPES INTO DATA(LS_FIELD_TYPE) WHERE KEYFLAG <> 'X'.
*              ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
*              IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_FIELD> IS NOT INITIAL.
*                " Update the existing record with the new value
*                ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_VALUE>).
*                IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
*                  <LV_EXISTING_VALUE> = <LV_FIELD>.
*                ENDIF.
*              ENDIF.
*            ENDLOOP.
            LOOP AT LT_FIELD_TYPES INTO DATA(LS_FIELD_TYPE) WHERE KEYFLAG <> 'X'.
              ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
              IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
                " Always update ALV field (even if Excel field is empty)
                ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_VALUE>).
                IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
                  <LV_EXISTING_VALUE> = COND #( WHEN <LV_FIELD> IS INITIAL THEN '' ELSE <LV_FIELD> ).
                ENDIF.
              ENDIF.
            ENDLOOP.

            " Mark the new record as not needed (we've updated the existing one)
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_DELETED' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
            IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
              <LV_FIELD> = 'X'.
            ENDIF.
            LV_DUPLICATE_FOUND = ABAP_TRUE.
            LV_UPDATE_COUNT = LV_UPDATE_COUNT + 1.
            EXIT. " Exit the inner loop once we've found and updated the matching record
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

      " If no duplicate found, count as insert
      IF LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_FALSE.
        LV_INSERT_COUNT = LV_INSERT_COUNT + 1.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Remove the records marked for deletion (the duplicates we've already processed)
  LOOP AT <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_ALV>.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_DELETED' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_FIELD> = 'X'.
      DELETE <LT_ALV> INDEX SY-TABIX.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " 8. Display results
  IF LV_DUPLICATE_FOUND = ABAP_TRUE.
    LV_MSG = |Processed { LV_ROW - 1 } records: Updated { LV_UPDATE_COUNT } existing records, Added { LV_INSERT_COUNT } new records|.
    MESSAGE LV_MSG TYPE 'S'.
  ELSE.
    LV_MSG = |Successfully added { LV_INSERT_COUNT } new records|.
    MESSAGE LV_MSG TYPE 'S'.
  ENDIF.

  " 9. Refresh ALV display
  GV_DATA_CHANGED = ABAP_TRUE.
  IF GO_GRID IS BOUND.
    CALL METHOD GO_GRID->REFRESH_TABLE_DISPLAY
      EXPORTING
        IS_STABLE = VALUE #( ROW = ABAP_TRUE COL = ABAP_TRUE ).
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: SAP B1 Crystal Report
entry.content: Can anyone help me design in crystal report for purchase quotation comparison like screenshot attached.I am using SAP B1 v10 HANA version. When I place vendors who quoted against purchase quotation in detail section, it displays vendors vertically in one row but my client mandates me to design in such a way that when they enter doc num for the purchase request, it should show all the vendors  horizontally and items(from purchase request) vertically. they want exactly same report as screenshot.Additonally there should sub columns like price ,total,BST under each vendor.I thought cross tab would work but I am not able to achieve it.I would be grateful if anyone help me on it

entry.title: Re: Tips and Tricks – Time Valuations with next day combinations (working or non working day filters
entry.content:
Hi @sanketgajre335  - Thanks for your valuable feedback and glad that you liked it

Considering you are one of the important, frequent and smarter users of our time tracking product, could you please let me know the use case where you need to check for the planned times of next day (or next day WS in general). In most of my cases so far, I have dealt with requirements where we usually check recorded times for next day (working or non working) for OT , Premium calculation etc.  Hence, I would be interested in knowing the use case/customer requirment where we need to check next day planned times and not working times. 

Best Regards

Neelesh

Neelesh
